informal_statement.text: Given a non-empty slice of integers, return the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices.
required_properties: the returned value equals the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices
there exist indices i <= j with i < arr.len() and j <= arr.len() such that arr[i..j] sums to the returned value
no contiguous non-empty subslice sums to a strictly greater value
edge_cases: all negative elements (max is the largest single element)
all positive elements (max is the whole slice)
a single large negative element interleaved among positives

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: the slice is non-empty.
common_failure_modes: treating the empty-slice case as returning 0

semantic_units.description: Existential witness: there is a contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned value.
common_failure_modes: stating only an upper bound without a witness
quantifying over subsequences rather than contiguous subslices

semantic_units.description: Upper-bound property: no contiguous non-empty subslice sums strictly above the returned value.
common_failure_modes: quantifying only over subslices of a fixed length

semantic_units.description: Contiguity constraint: the witness is a contiguous range i..j, not a subsequence.
common_failure_modes: allowing gaps in the witnessed indices

semantic_units.description: All-negative case: the result equals the maximum single element when every element is negative.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr), arr ≠ [] → ∃ (i j : Nat), i ≤ j ∧ j ≤ arr.length ∧ i < j ∧ sliceSum arr i j = maxSubarray arr
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: For any non-empty input, there exists a contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned maximum.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (i j : Nat), arr ≠ [] → i < j → j ≤ arr.length → sliceSum arr i j ≤ maxSubarray arr
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: For any non-empty input, no contiguous non-empty subslice has a sum strictly greater than the returned maximum.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
import Std

open scoped BigOperators

def maxContigSubsliceSum (xs : Array Int) : Int :=
  if h : xs.length = 0 then 0 else
  let n := xs.length
  let sums : Fin n → Int := fun i =>
    let recSum : Nat → Int := fun len =>
      let j : Nat := i.1 + len
      if h' : j ≤ n then
        (xs.extract i.1 (len)).foldl (· + ·) 0
      else 0
    recSum 0
  -- placeholder; actual implementation obligations below
  0
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the target function that returns the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices of a non-empty array of integers (with a harmless value for the empty case).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem maxContigSubsliceSum_spec_pre {xs : Array Int} (hxs : xs.length > 0) : True := by trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input slice/array is non-empty (length > 0).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem exists_nonempty_subslice {xs : Array Int} (hxs : xs.length > 0) :
  ∃ (i j : Nat), i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length := by
    refine ⟨0, 1, ?_, ?_⟩
    · exact Nat.zero_lt_one
    · simpa [hxs] using (Nat.succ_le_iff.2 (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero (by
        intro h0; simpa [h0] using hxs)))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: For a non-empty array, there exists at least one contiguous non-empty subslice (choose i=0, j=1).
Define the sum of a contiguous subslice xs[i..j) and note that if i<j then the subslice is non-empty.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSubsliceSum_upper_bound {xs : Array Int} (hxs : xs.length > 0)
    (i j : Nat) (hij : i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length) :
    subsliceSum xs i j ≤ maxContigSubsliceSum xs hxs := by
  -- proof obligation: the returned value is at least every subslice sum
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned maximum is greater than or equal to the sum of any contiguous non-empty subslice.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSubsliceSum_attains {xs : Array Int} (hxs : xs.length > 0) :
  ∃ (i j : Nat), i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length ∧
    maxContigSubsliceSum xs hxs = subsliceSum xs i j := by
  -- proof obligation: the maximum is achieved by some contiguous non-empty subslice
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists a contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned maximum.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSubsliceSum_characterization {xs : Array Int} (hxs : xs.length > 0) :
  maxContigSubsliceSum xs hxs =
    (Finset.univ.image (fun p : {p : Nat × Nat // p.1 < p.2 ∧ p.2 ≤ xs.length} =>
      subsliceSum xs p.1.1 p.1.2)).sup (by
        -- non-emptiness of the set of valid subslices
        have : ∃ p : {p : Nat × Nat // p.1 < p.2 ∧ p.2 ≤ xs.length}, True := by
          obtain ⟨i, j, hi_lt, hj_le⟩ := ⟨0, 1, Nat.zero_lt_one, Nat.succ_le_iff.2 (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero (by
            intro h0; simpa [h0] using hxs)))⟩
          refine ⟨⟨(i, j), ?_⟩, trivial⟩
          exact ⟨hi_lt, hj_le⟩
        simpa [Finset.nonempty_iff_ne_empty] using this) := by
  -- proof obligation: returned value equals the supremum/maximum over all valid subslice sums
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned value is exactly the maximum (supremum over a finite set) of the sums of all contiguous non-empty subslices.
